    Product6 min read

    How to Improve Video Upload Reliability and Performance

    theRec.site Founder · Last reviewed: June 2026

    Why uploads sometimes slow down and how multipart transfer, retries, and network habits keep large files moving.

    Large video files stress home and office networks. Upload performance depends on bandwidth, Wi-Fi stability, VPN routing, and concurrent meetings. Recording at reasonable resolution and frame rate reduces file size without sacrificing clarity for most business use cases.

    Multipart upload splits a file into chunks so a failed segment can retry without restarting the entire transfer. theRec.site uses this approach to improve reliability. If upload stalls, wait a few minutes, avoid closing the tab, and ensure sleep mode will not interrupt the session.

    Wired connections and off-peak upload times help for very long sessions. If you routinely upload hour-long trainings, consider breaking them into chapters so viewers digest content more easily and each part uploads faster.

    Track progress indicators and save recording IDs if you contact support. Proactive network habits—stable connection, updated browser, sufficient plan storage—prevent most upload issues before they block your workflow.

    Key takeaways

    • Right-size resolution for business recordings
    • Multipart upload enables partial retries
    • Stable connections prevent unnecessary failures
    • Split very long content into shorter chapters
